description_long: This is a medium-sized wooden cabin with a gable roof covered in reddish-brown hexagonal shingles. The front facade features a set of double doors at the center, flanked by two sets of rectangular windows with multiple panes. The side walls each have three evenly spaced windows, while the rear wall displays a symmetrical arrangement of four windows. The cabin is elevated slightly above the ground on concrete footings, and the exterior walls are constructed from light-colored timber planks with visible interlocking corners. The overall design is simple and functional, with a rustic yet orderly appearance suitable for residential or recreational use.
description: A wooden cabin with a gable roof, central double doors, and multiple rectangular windows, elevated on concrete footings.
description_view_0: Front view showing central double doors and two sets of multi-pane windows on either side.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the solid wooden wall with no windows or doors, and the gable roof's end.
description_view_2: Rear view with four symmetrically arranged multi-pane windows and no doors.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing three evenly spaced multi-pane windows and the gable roof's end.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate pitch, covered in reddish-brown hexagonal asphalt shingles and slight overhangs
attribute_window_arrangement: Multiple rectangular windows with multi-pane design; two on the front facade flanking the door, three evenly spaced on one side, and four symmetrically arranged on the rear wall
attribute_door_placement: One set of double doors centered on the front facade, directly between the two front windows
